questions.a: COLHYBRI to platforma inkluzji finansowej, która łączy osoby wykluczone z systemu bankowego z lokalnymi sklepami poprzez przystępną subskrypcję. Ceny zostaną ogłoszone wkrótce. COLHYBRI jest zaprojektowane tak, aby było przystępne zarówno dla osób prywatnych, jak i lokalnych sklepów. Nie będzie ukrytych opłat, sprawdzania zdolności kredytowej ani wymagań dotyczących minimalnego salda.

questions.q: Czy potrzebuję konta bankowego, aby korzystać z COLHYBRI?
questions.a: Nie. COLHYBRI jest specjalnie zaprojektowane dla osób wykluczonych lub częściowo wykluczonych z systemu bankowego. Możesz korzystać z usług finansowych przez uczestniczące lokalne sklepy w swojej okolicy.
COLHYBRI startuje w Polsce w 2026 roku. Polska została wybrana ze względu na populację osób wykluczonych z systemu bankowego, tradycję spółdzielczości oraz dynamiczny ekosystem lokalnego handlu.
COLHYBRI zostało stworzone i jest kierowane przez założyciela i CEO Florent Gibert.

Mnożnik keynesowski opisuje, w jaki sposób pieniądze wydane lokalnie generują dodatkową aktywność gospodarczą. W przypadku COLHYBRI każda subskrypcja tworzy zwielokrotnioną wartość ekonomiczną, gdy pieniądze krążą w społeczności.

revitalisation-centres-villes.title: Downtown revitalization in France
revitalisation-centres-villes.metaDescription: 9B EUR mobilized by Action Coeur de Ville and Petites Villes de Demain for 1,890 towns. Complete guide to commercial revitalization in France.
revitalisation-centres-villes.snippet: France mobilizes 9 billion EUR for downtown revitalization through Action Coeur de Ville (244 midsize towns, 5B EUR) and Petites Villes de Demain (1,646 small towns, 4B EUR). In 2026, 1,732 towns are classified as downtown revitalization zones (ZRCV) per the December 26, 2025 decree (ANCT).

sections.body: Downtown revitalization refers to public policies and operational tools that revive commercial cores of towns suffering from vacancy, population decline and service loss. It is built around three levers: housing, local commerce and public services. France's ANCT coordinates national programs alongside Banque des Territoires.

sections.h2: What are the national programs in France?
sections.body: Two programs dominate: Action Coeur de Ville (244 midsize towns, 5B EUR over 2023-2026) and Petites Villes de Demain (1,646 towns under 20,000 inhabitants, 4B EUR). A third tool, ZRCV tax zones, exempts downtown merchants from local business and property taxes. 1,732 towns are classified ZRCV in 2026.

sections.h2: How does COLHYBRI contribute to revitalization?
sections.body: COLHYBRI provides the missing layer: a SaaS tool to measure downtown vitality in real time. Maps+ audits every shop Google presence in 48 hours, the community pool drives foot traffic to partner merchants and the municipal dashboard delivers verifiable metrics to the city council. A 30-minute scoping call lets a city plan the turnkey rollout on 50 shops.

faq.a: Public policies that revive commercial cores through housing, commerce and services. In France it is coordinated by ANCT through the Action Coeur de Ville and Petites Villes de Demain programs.

faq.q: What are the national programs in France?
faq.a: Action Coeur de Ville (244 towns, 5B EUR, 2023-2026), Petites Villes de Demain (1,646 towns, 4B EUR), and the ZRCV tax zones covering 1,732 towns in 2026.

faq.q: How does COLHYBRI help a city in practice?
faq.a: Turnkey deployment: Google audit of 50 shops, municipal vitality dashboard, city council impact report and deployment support.

action-coeur-de-ville.title: Action Coeur de Ville
action-coeur-de-ville.h1: Action Coeur de Ville: revitalizing French midsize towns
action-coeur-de-ville.metaTitle: Action Coeur de Ville: 244 towns, 5B EUR, 2023-2026
action-coeur-de-ville.metaDescription: France's Action Coeur de Ville program mobilizes 5B EUR to revitalize 244 midsize towns. Led by ANCT with Banque des Territoires, Action Logement and Anah.
action-coeur-de-ville.snippet: Action Coeur de Ville is a national program launched in 2018 mobilizing 5 billion EUR over 2023-2026 to revitalize 244 French midsize towns. Led by ANCT with Banque des Territoires (2.5B EUR), Action Logement (1.7B EUR) and Anah (1B EUR), it covers housing renovation, commercial return and digital transition.

sections.h2: What is the Action Coeur de Ville program?
sections.body: Action Coeur de Ville (ACV) is the flagship French program for revitalizing midsize towns. Launched in 2018, it now includes 244 towns and 20 million inhabitants. Scope covers five axes: housing, commerce, accessibility, public space, digital transition. Financing crosses Banque des Territoires, Action Logement and Anah.

The 244 ACV towns include departmental capitals and sub-prefectures facing commercial decline. Flagship beneficiaries include Nimes, Chalon-sur-Saone, Draguignan, Montlucon, Roanne and Saint-Brieuc. Each town gets a multi-year agreement, dedicated project lead and priority access to ANCT funding.

sections.h2: How does a shop benefit from ACV?
sections.body: A shop in the ACV perimeter can access storefront renovation grants, rent subsidies, digital premiums and complementary Petites Villes de Demain support. COLHYBRI lets the ACV commerce manager pilot digital visibility of every shop through a single municipal dashboard.

faq.q: What is Action Coeur de Ville?
faq.a: A French national program launched in 2018 mobilizing 5B EUR to revitalize 244 midsize towns, led by ANCT with Banque des Territoires, Action Logement and Anah.
244 towns including Nimes, Chalon-sur-Saone, Roanne, Saint-Brieuc, Montlucon. Full list available on the ANCT website.
COLHYBRI provides ACV commerce managers with a real-time municipal vitality dashboard and audits every shop Google presence in 48 hours.

petites-villes-de-demain.title: Petites Villes de Demain
petites-villes-de-demain.h1: Petites Villes de Demain: revitalizing French small towns
petites-villes-de-demain.metaTitle: Petites Villes de Demain: 1,646 towns, 4B EUR
petites-villes-de-demain.metaDescription: The Petites Villes de Demain program supports 1,646 small towns with 4B EUR. Led by ANCT, covering housing, commerce and services.
petites-villes-de-demain.snippet: The Petites Villes de Demain program (PVD), led by ANCT, supports 1,646 French towns under 20,000 inhabitants in their revitalization. With 4 billion EUR committed through 2026, it funds infrastructure, services and quality of life projects reaching over 7 million inhabitants (ANCT, 2026).

sections.h2: What is the Petites Villes de Demain program?
sections.body: PVD is a national initiative launched in 2020 targeting 1,646 small towns at the center of their local living area. It complements Action Coeur de Ville to form a complete territorial revitalization chain. Each town signs an ORT convention with its inter-municipal body and ANCT.

Towns were selected by ANCT and regional prefects on centrality and fragility criteria. The list has been fixed since 2021 and new calls are rare. PVD is now in its operational deployment phase through end-2026.

COLHYBRI is the operational tool for measuring commercial vitality for PVD project leads. Turnkey rollout on 50 shops, municipal dashboard, impact report. Especially suited to small towns without a dedicated commerce manager.

faq.q: What is the Petites Villes de Demain program?
faq.a: A French national program launched in 2020 supporting 1,646 small towns with 4B EUR through 2026, led by ANCT.
By ANCT and regional prefects on centrality and fragility criteria. The list has been fixed since 2021; new calls are rare.

faq.q: How does COLHYBRI help a PVD town?
faq.a: Turnkey deployment: Google audit of 50 shops, real-time vitality dashboard, city council impact report.

zrcv-zones-revitalisation.title: ZRCV downtown revitalization zones
zrcv-zones-revitalisation.h1: ZRCV: French downtown revitalization tax zones explained
zrcv-zones-revitalisation.metaDescription: France's ZRCV tax zones exempt downtown shops from local business tax and property tax. 1,732 towns classified in 2026.
zrcv-zones-revitalisation.snippet: Downtown revitalization zones (ZRCV) let French municipalities exempt their downtown shops from local business tax (CFE) and property tax. In 2026, 1,732 towns are classified ZRCV under the December 26, 2025 decree. The tool remains underused: many towns are unaware they are eligible.

sections.body: A downtown revitalization zone is a fiscal perimeter defined by the municipality. Inside the zone, shops benefit from exemption on Cotisation Fonciere des Entreprises (CFE) and property tax for 5 years. The 2018 Pinel law extended the scheme, and the December 26, 2025 decree updated the list.

sections.h2: How to know if my town is a ZRCV?
sections.body: The official list is published in the French Journal Officiel. The December 26, 2025 decree classifies 1,732 towns. Criteria include population, commercial vacancy rate, demographic pressure and belonging to an ACV or PVD program. The DGE publishes the interactive map.

sections.h2: How does COLHYBRI help a ZRCV town?
sections.body: COLHYBRI complements the fiscal tool with operational measurement: 48-hour Google audit of shops in the perimeter, vitality dashboard, city council impact report. Combining tax exemption with a digital tool maximizes revitalization success.

faq.a: A French downtown revitalization zone: a fiscal perimeter where shops are exempt from CFE and property tax for 5 years, created by the 2018 Pinel law.
1,732 French towns are classified under the December 26, 2025 decree. The official list is in the Journal Officiel and on the DGE website.
Any shop inside the ZRCV perimeter defined by the municipality, subject to revenue and activity conditions set by the 2018 Pinel law.

manager-commerce-cci.title: Commerce manager (CCI)
manager-commerce-cci.h1: Commerce manager: the job that revitalizes French downtowns
manager-commerce-cci.metaTitle: Commerce manager CCI: mission and tools 2026
manager-commerce-cci.metaDescription: Commerce managers funded by French Chambers of Commerce are the operational backbone of downtown revitalization. The government co-funds the role since 2025.
manager-commerce-cci.snippet: Commerce managers, funded by French Chambers of Commerce and Industry (CCI), are the operational backbone of downtown revitalization. In 2025, the French government announced co-funding for commerce manager positions as part of the national commerce plan.

sections.body: A commerce manager is a municipal or inter-municipal professional whose mission is to animate and coordinate the commercial fabric of a town. Tasks include: identifying vacant units, supporting new project leaders, organizing commercial events, tracking foot traffic and engaging with private landlords.

Historically, CCI funded the role on their own budget. Since 2025, a State-CCI-city co-funding scheme has been launched as part of the national commerce plan. Action Coeur de Ville and Petites Villes de Demain cities get priority access.

sections.h2: How does a commerce manager use COLHYBRI?
sections.body: COLHYBRI is the commerce manager digital toolkit. The municipal dashboard tracks each shop Score Maps in real time, flags incomplete Google listings, prompts shops lacking recent reviews and measures the impact of actions on commercial vitality.

faq.a: A municipal or inter-municipal professional who animates and coordinates the commercial fabric. Missions cover vacant units, new project support and foot traffic tracking.
Historically CCI; since 2025 a State-CCI-city co-funding scheme. ACV and PVD cities get priority access.

faq.q: How does COLHYBRI help a commerce manager?
faq.a: A municipal vitality dashboard, 48-hour automated Google audit and a city council impact report.

vacance-commerciale-france.title: Commercial vacancy in France
vacance-commerciale-france.h1: Commercial vacancy in France: figures, causes and solutions 2026
vacance-commerciale-france.metaTitle: Commercial vacancy in France 2026: 55% of midsize towns
vacance-commerciale-france.metaDescription: 55% of French midsize towns have commercial vacancy rates above 10%, up 4 points in 15 years (CCI France). Analysis and solutions.
vacance-commerciale-france.snippet: Commercial vacancy affects 55% of French midsize towns with a rate above 10%, up 4 points in 15 years (CCI France, 2024). Main causes are online platform competition, commercial rent pressure, administrative constraints and evolving consumer habits toward peripheral retail.

sections.h2: What is the commercial vacancy rate in France?
sections.body: According to CCI France, 55% of midsize French towns have a vacancy rate above 10% in 2024, versus 51% in 2009. Some towns exceed 20% vacancy: Beziers, Vierzon, Agen. Major cities are largely spared (6-8% average) but small and midsize downtowns are the hardest hit.

Four main causes: online platform competition (Amazon, Shein), commercial rent pressure that remains high even during vacancy, administrative constraints (permits, accessibility, standards) and the shift of consumer habits toward peripheral retail and drive-through.

Downtown revitalization combines several levers: fiscal (ZRCV), operational (commerce manager), architectural (storefront renovation) and digital (online visibility). COLHYBRI delivers the digital pillar: 48-hour Google audit and community pool driving traffic to remaining shops.

faq.q: What is the commercial vacancy rate in France?
faq.a: 55% of midsize towns have rates above 10%, up 4 points in 15 years per CCI France. Some towns exceed 20%: Beziers, Vierzon, Agen.
Online platform competition, commercial rent pressure, administrative constraints and consumer shift toward peripheral retail.

faq.q: How to reduce vacancy in my town?
faq.a: Combining fiscal (ZRCV), operational (commerce manager), architectural and digital (COLHYBRI). Turnkey deployment available.

municipales-2026-commerce.title: 2026 French municipal elections
municipales-2026-commerce.h1: 2026 French municipal elections: downtown as the top issue
municipales-2026-commerce.metaTitle: 2026 French municipal elections: downtown, issue number 1
municipales-2026-commerce.metaDescription: The 2026 French municipal elections put downtown revitalization at the top of the agenda. The Confederation of French Merchants published a White Paper.
municipales-2026-commerce.snippet: Ahead of the 2026 French municipal elections, downtown revitalization has become the central issue for mandate programs. The Confederation of French Merchants (CDF) published a White Paper proposing solutions around four axes: vacant shops, soft mobility, commercial animation and security.

sections.h2: Why is commerce the top issue for the 2026 municipals?
sections.body: After 15 years of rising commercial vacancy, French downtowns have become the visible symbol of territorial decline. Candidates in the 2026 municipals must answer a strong voter demand: bring the main street back to life. Polls rank commerce first among local concerns, ahead of security and mobility.

sections.h2: What does the Confederation of French Merchants propose?
sections.body: The CDF published a 2026 municipal elections White Paper with four axes: reduce commercial vacancy (via ZRCV and rent oversight), encourage soft mobility (targeted pedestrianization), animate downtowns (events, markets) and reinforce security (police presence, lighting). Available on the CDF website.

sections.h2: How does COLHYBRI help a candidate build a program?
sections.body: COLHYBRI provides candidates with an objective diagnosis of downtown commercial vitality: vacancy rate, average Score Maps, incomplete Google listings, shops without recent reviews. The turnkey 4-week rollout lets candidates present a concrete action plan with before/after metrics.

faq.q: Why is commerce central to the 2026 municipals?
faq.a: After 15 years of rising commercial vacancy, commerce has become the visible symbol of territorial decline. It tops local concerns in polls.
A White Paper with 4 axes: vacancy, soft mobility, animation, security. Available on the CDF website.

faq.q: How does COLHYBRI help a candidate?

downtown-revitalization.snippet: Downtown revitalization in the United States is powered by Opportunity Zones (8,764 designated tracts), Main Street America (1,200+ communities) and federal RAISE grants totaling hundreds of millions of dollars. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 makes Opportunity Zones permanent and launches OZ 2.0 nominations in July 2026.

sections.h2: What programs drive downtown revitalization in the USA?
sections.body: Three programs dominate: Opportunity Zones (federal tax tool created in 2017, made permanent in 2025), Main Street America (a network of 1,200+ communities using preservation-based economic development), and RAISE grants from the Department of Transportation ($23.7M for East Moline, $20M for Allentown). Historic tax credits (20% federal + 25% state) complete the toolkit.

sections.h2: What is the One Big Beautiful Bill Act?
sections.body: Signed in 2025, the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) makes Opportunity Zones permanent and launches a new OZ 2.0 designation in July 2026. The income threshold drops from 80% to 70% of median family income, reducing eligible tracts by about 20%. Rural OZ investments qualify for up to 30% capital gains reduction.

sections.h2: How does COLHYBRI complement these programs?
sections.body: COLHYBRI is the SaaS that makes Main Street measurable. The municipal dashboard delivers real-time commercial vitality metrics, ideal for justifying Opportunity Zone investments or federal grant renewals. Turnkey rollout on 50 shops, funded by residents at $3/month. No commitment from the city.

faq.a: Opportunity Zones (8,764 tracts), Main Street America (1,200+ communities), DOT RAISE grants, historic tax credits (20% federal + 25% state).
OZ 2.0 launches in July 2026 via the OBBBA 2025. New 70% median family income threshold and 30% capital gains reduction for rural tracts.

opportunity-zones.title: Opportunity Zones 2.0
opportunity-zones.metaDescription: Opportunity Zones 2.0, established by OBBBA 2025, redesignate census tracts starting July 2026. New 70% threshold, 20% fewer eligible tracts.
opportunity-zones.snippet: Opportunity Zones 2.0, established by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025, redesignate all census tracts starting July 2026 with new eligibility criteria. The income threshold drops from 80% to 70% of median family income, reducing eligible tracts by approximately 20%. Rural OZ investments qualify for up to 30% capital gains reduction.

sections.body: An Opportunity Zone is a census tract designated as distressed where investors can place capital gains in exchange for substantial tax benefits: deferral, 10-15% reduction if held 5-7 years, and full exemption on gains from the OZ investment itself if held 10+ years. The tool was created by the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed in 2025 makes Opportunity Zones permanent and launches a full redesignation in July 2026. The income threshold drops from 80% to 70% of median family income, reducing eligible tracts by about 20%. Rural OZ investments qualify for up to 30% capital gains reduction, up from 10%.

Cities must identify census tracts likely to be redesignated, document revitalization potential and build attractive investment cases. COLHYBRI provides an objective commercial vitality diagnosis with per-shop Score Maps, an ideal metric to justify OZ 2.0 investment.

main-street-america.title: Main Street America
main-street-america.h1: Main Street America: How 1,200+ Communities Are Revitalizing Their Downtowns
main-street-america.metaDescription: Main Street America is a nationwide network of 1,200+ communities using preservation-based economic development to revitalize historic commercial districts.
main-street-america.snippet: Main Street America is a nationwide network of over 1,200 communities using a preservation-based economic development approach to revitalize historic commercial districts. The Four-Point Approach covers organization, promotion, design, and economic restructuring. COLHYBRI integrates as the digital measurement layer for Main Street programs.

sections.body: Main Street America is a program launched in 1980 by the National Trust for Historic Preservation. Today, more than 1,200 American communities participate, covering small rural towns, historic urban neighborhoods and midsize downtowns. Each community designates a Main Street Manager and applies the Four-Point Approach.

The Four-Point Approach covers four domains: organization (stakeholder coalitions, volunteers, funding), promotion (events, marketing, retail recruitment), design (facade renovation, streetscape, architectural preservation), and economic restructuring (shop support, tenant attraction). A proven model over 40+ years.

sections.h2: How does COLHYBRI complement Main Street?
sections.body: COLHYBRI is the Main Street Manager digital toolkit. It provides per-shop Score Maps on the Main Street, a real-time commercial vitality dashboard, and a quarterly impact report for the board. It integrates the economic restructuring pillar with objective metrics.

faq.a: A program launched in 1980 by the National Trust for Historic Preservation. Over 1,200 American communities participate using the Four-Point Approach.

rust-belt-revitalization.snippet: Rust Belt cities like Pittsburgh, Cleveland, Canton and Allentown are transforming from heavy manufacturing to tech, healthcare and mixed-use downtown development. Canton, Ohio serves as a blueprint: one developer restored 42 buildings using historic tax credits (20% federal + 25% state). Allentown received a $20 million EDA grant for small-scale manufacturing on brownfield sites.

sections.body: The Rust Belt refers to the declining industrial belt of the United States, from the Northeast to the Midwest: Pennsylvania, Ohio, Michigan, Indiana, Illinois. Hit by deindustrialization since the 1970s, the region is in full transformation in 2026, driven by federal investments (CHIPS Act, Inflation Reduction Act) and Opportunity Zones.

sections.h2: How does Canton Ohio revitalize its downtown?
sections.body: Canton has become the Rust Belt revitalization model. Local developer Steve Coon restored 42 historic downtown buildings using federal historic tax credits (20%) plus Ohio state historic credits (25%), stacking 45% in combined credits. Downtown went from abandoned district to mixed-use destination in under 10 years.

sections.h2: What tools do Rust Belt cities use?
sections.body: A combination of federal and local tools: historic tax credits (20% federal + 25% state), Opportunity Zones, RAISE grants from the Department of Transportation ($20M for Allentown), and Main Street program support. COLHYBRI adds the digital layer to measure impact in real time.

sections.body: French mutualism originated in 1806 with the creation of the first mutual aid societies. These structures let workers pool resources to face illness, disability and unemployment. From this movement came Social Security (1945) and the large mutuelles of today. 220 years of tradition shape the French social landscape.

Digital mutualism brings these principles into the digital economy: modest contribution, shared risk, redistribution to members. COLHYBRI applies this model to local commerce: 3 EUR/month per resident, 75% to the solidarity pool, services randomly gifted at partner shops. This is not charity; it is a mutual.

sections.h2: Why is digital mutualism fit for 2026?
sections.body: It answers 2026 expectations: 79% of French people aspire to more brotherhood (IFOP, 2025) but 78% do not trust fellow citizens. Mutualization without an intermediary (no bank, no NGO) resolves this paradox by creating a light, transparent frame where solidarity happens without risk.

sections.body: Caffe sospeso is a tradition born in Naples bars in the early 20th century. A customer who had a lucky day pays for two coffees instead of one: they consume the first and leave the second "suspended" at the bar for a stranger who might need it. It is an anonymous act of solidarity that does not humiliate anyone, neither giver nor receiver.

sections.h2: Why does COLHYBRI draw on it?
sections.body: Caffe sospeso perfectly illustrates the COLHYBRI model: anonymous solidarity, modest gesture, no intermediary. COLHYBRI extends the principle: instead of offering only coffees, it lets people offer any service from a partner shop (hairdresser, dry cleaner, bakery, yoga class). Digitalization removes friction while preserving the spirit.

sections.h2: How does it work in practice?
sections.body: Each COLHYBRI subscriber pays 3 EUR/month. 75% of that sum feeds a neighborhood solidarity pool. Each month, the pool is redistributed as services randomly gifted at partner shops. A subscriber can both give (through their contribution) and receive (through a gifted service). It is caffe sospeso, 2026 edition.

sections.body: A solidarity pool is a common fund fed by several members contributions and redistributed according to a transparent rule. COLHYBRI chose a simple rule: each month, beneficiaries are drawn at random from neighborhood subscribers. All have equal chances, all contribute at the same level. It is the opposite of a privilege or clientelism logic.

Beneficiaries are drawn at random by a transparent and verifiable algorithm each month. A subscriber can be drawn multiple times per year, or never. This randomness ensures fairness and creates a positive surprise effect. The selection is public: every subscriber can verify the draw took place.

75% of subscriptions feed the redistributed pool. The remaining 25% cover platform technical costs (hosting, support, development), operational costs (accounting, legal) and COLHYBRI margin. It is a transparent ratio that lets the model run sustainably without subsidies or donations.

avis-google-commercants.metaDescription: A shop with 10+ recent reviews gets 50% more clicks. COLHYBRI method: post-visit QR code to generate authentic reviews.
avis-google-commercants.snippet: Google reviews are the top selection criterion for consumers choosing a local shop. The COLHYBRI method uses a post-visit QR code that simplifies review submission: the customer scans the code on their way out, rates in 1 click, and the shop accumulates authentic reviews effortlessly. A shop with 10+ recent reviews gets 50% more clicks.

sections.h2: Why are Google reviews so important?
sections.body: 87% of consumers read online reviews before visiting a local shop (BrightLocal, 2024). A shop with fewer than 5 reviews inspires distrust; a shop with more than 20 recent reviews inspires confidence. Google uses review quantity, quality and recency as ranking signals in the Local Pack. Reviews are both a trust signal and an SEO signal.

sections.h2: How to get more reviews without asking?
sections.body: The COLHYBRI method relies on a post-visit QR code printed on the receipt or displayed at the exit. The customer scans the code with their smartphone, lands directly on the Google Business review page, and rates in 1 click without creating an account. This simplification triples the conversion rate compared to a verbal request.

COLHYBRI activates continuous monitoring: any new alert (positive or negative) is sent to the merchant within 2 hours. This allows merchants to respond quickly to negative reviews, propose a solution and reduce the ranking impact. A negative review answered quickly does less harm than a negative review ignored for 3 months.

faq.a: 87% of consumers read reviews before visiting a local shop. Google uses quantity, quality and recency as ranking signals.

faq.q: How to get more reviews without asking?
faq.a: Post-visit QR code on the receipt. Customer scans, lands on review page, rates in 1 click. Conversion rate x3 vs verbal request.
Continuous monitoring: alert sent within 2 hours for any new review. Allows fast response and solution offering.

onboarding-digital-commerce.title: Zero-friction merchant onboarding
onboarding-digital-commerce.h1: Zero-friction digital onboarding for merchants: the COLHYBRI method
onboarding-digital-commerce.metaTitle: Zero-friction merchant onboarding: 30 seconds, QR code
onboarding-digital-commerce.metaDescription: COLHYBRI lets a merchant become visible on Google in 30 seconds. QR code, no account, no technical skills required.
onboarding-digital-commerce.snippet: COLHYBRI zero-friction digital onboarding lets any merchant become visible on Google in under 30 seconds. The process: scan a QR code, confirm pre-filled information (name, address, phone), and accept creation or optimization of the Google Business listing. No technical skills required.

sections.body: Digital platforms usually ask merchants to create an account, fill a long form, upload documents and verify a postal address. A 55-year-old merchant without a tech background typically abandons at step 3. COLHYBRI zero-friction onboarding removes these frictions: one QR scan, one confirmation, done.

Step 1: the merchant scans a QR code distributed by the municipal commerce manager or by a neighbor merchant. Step 2: they land on a web page pre-filled with information from public registries, existing Google Business, or the municipal ACV/PVD database. Step 3: they validate in 1 click and the listing is created or optimized automatically.

COLHYBRI automatically queries several public sources: public business registries (name, address, status), existing Google Business Profile (if present), and the municipal database of the ACV or PVD program (if the city is a partner). The merchant just has to verify and correct if needed.

faq.a: Classic platforms lose merchants at step 3. 30-second onboarding removes friction and lets non-technical merchants go digital.
QR code scan, confirmation of pre-filled info, 1-click validation. No account to create, no documents to upload.
COLHYBRI queries public registries, existing Google Business and the municipal ACV/PVD database. The merchant just verifies.

score-maps-audit.title: Score Maps audit
score-maps-audit.h1: Score Maps: Google presence audit for local shops
score-maps-audit.metaTitle: COLHYBRI Score Maps: 0-100 Google presence audit
score-maps-audit.metaDescription: Score Maps measures Google presence quality on a 0-100 scale. 4 criteria: listing, reviews, photos, NAP consistency.
score-maps-audit.snippet: Score Maps is an indicator developed by COLHYBRI that measures the quality of a shop Google presence on a 0 to 100 scale. It evaluates four criteria: listing completeness (30%), review quantity and quality (30%), number of photos (20%), and NAP consistency (20%). A score below 50 means the shop is nearly invisible online.

sections.body: Score Maps sums four weighted components: Google Business listing completeness (30% - category, hours, description, attributes), review quantity and quality (30% - count, average rating, recency, response rate), photo count and quality (20% - exterior, interior, products), NAP consistency across main directories (20%). Each component is scored 0-100.

sections.h2: What do the different score levels mean?
sections.body: 0-30: shop nearly invisible, listing missing or abandoned, no reviews. 30-50: partially visible, existing but incomplete listing. 50-70: visible, significant improvement potential. 70-90: well-ranked, solid Local Pack positioning. 90-100: optimal, local category leader.

sections.h2: How does Score Maps help a city?
sections.body: At municipal level, the average Score Maps of downtown shops is a commercial vitality indicator. A city can compare its average to neighbors and track its evolution over time. COLHYBRI provides a municipal dashboard displaying every shop Score Maps plus the city average with per-street visualization.

faq.a: 4 weighted components: listing completeness (30%), reviews (30%), photos (20%), NAP consistency (20%). Each scored 0-100.
0-30: invisible. 30-50: incomplete. 50-70: visible with potential. 70-90: well-ranked. 90-100: optimal.

faq.q: How does it help a city?
faq.a: Aggregated commercial vitality indicator. Comparison with neighbors and time-based tracking. Municipal dashboard with per-street view.

maps-plus-outil.title: Maps+ COLHYBRI tool
maps-plus-outil.h1: Maps+ by COLHYBRI: the digitalization tool for local shops
maps-plus-outil.metaTitle: Maps+ COLHYBRI: audit, onboarding, reviews, monitoring
maps-plus-outil.metaDescription: Maps+ is the COLHYBRI digitalization module. 4 pillars: 48h Score Maps audit, zero-friction onboarding, QR review generation, monitoring.
maps-plus-outil.snippet: Maps+ is the COLHYBRI digitalization module that covers the 4 pillars of online shop visibility: automated audit (48-hour Score Maps), zero-friction Google Places onboarding, review generation via post-visit QR code, and continuous monitoring with negative review alerts. Included in the 10 EUR/month merchant subscription.

sections.h2: What does Maps+ do in practice?
sections.body: Maps+ is a SaaS module that automates all digital tasks for a shop: audit of Google presence (with Score Maps), creation or optimization of the Google Business listing, customer review generation via QR code printed on the receipt, and continuous monitoring with SMS or email alerts on negative reviews or ranking drops.

Maps+ is included in the COLHYBRI merchant subscription at 10 EUR/month. For comparison, local SEO agencies charge 200-500 EUR/month for equivalent services, Yelp charges $300-1000/month for advertising, and commission platforms take 25-30% on every sale. Maps+ is 10-50x cheaper while being automated.

sections.h2: How does Maps+ integrate with a municipal program?
sections.body: For Action Coeur de Ville, Petites Villes de Demain or Main Street America cities, Maps+ integrates with the municipal dashboard. The commerce manager can track Score Maps of every shop in their perimeter, detect struggling shops (score < 50), and trigger targeted support actions. Quarterly impact report ready for the city council.

faq.q: What does Maps+ do in practice?
faq.a: 4 pillars: 48-hour Score Maps audit, Google Business listing creation/optimization, QR code review generation, continuous monitoring with alerts.
Included in merchant subscription. Local SEO agencies charge 200-500 EUR/month, Yelp $300-1000/month. Maps+ is 10-50x cheaper and automated.

faq.q: How does it integrate with a municipal program?

espana.title: Barcelona, Capital Europea del Comerc de Proximitat 2026.
espana.subtitle: El pequeno comercio espanol genera 1,01 billones de euros al ano (INE 2023). Barcelona demuestra que la proximidad puede convertirse en estandar europeo de resiliencia urbana. COLHYBRI acompana este movimiento.

context: Barcelona fue designada Capital Europea del Comerc de Proximitat 2026 el 28 de enero de 2026 en Bruselas, superando a Zaragoza y Utrecht. La iniciativa fue impulsada desde 2019 por Barcelona Comerc y Vitrines d'Europe, asociaciones privadas de comerciantes, ratificada por el Parlamento Europeo en enero de 2023 y cofinanciada por la Comision Europea (DG GROW) a traves de EISMEA.

La crisis del pequeno comercio espanol es real: de los 300 000 pequenos comercios que habia en Espana en 2010, entre el 30% y el 40% han desaparecido en 2025. Solo en 2025 cerraron 13 586 comercios (una media de 1 132 al mes segun UPTA), y las proyecciones apuntan a mas de 12 000 cierres adicionales en 2026 si no cambian las politicas de apoyo.

Cataluna representa el 19,5% del comercio espanol (24,9% la Comunidad de Madrid, segun INE). El modelo Barcelona demuestra que el comercio de proximidad no es solo paisaje urbano ni tradicion, sino estructura productiva y motor de cohesion social. COLHYBRI es el soporte digital nativo de este modelo, replicable en cualquier municipio espanol o europeo.

uk.subtitle: The High Streets Task Force proved that revitalization works when local partnerships get the right tools. The 1.5 billion pound Plan for Neighbourhoods meets its missing digital layer.

stats.label: UK high street shops currently vacant (1 in 7)
High Streets supported by the Task Force 2019-2024
UK Plan for Neighbourhoods + High Street Rental Auctions budget

context: The UK Government's High Streets Task Force (HSTF) operated from 2019 to 2024 under the leadership of the Institute of Place Management (Manchester Metropolitan University), working with the Royal Town Planning Institute, Design Council, PwC, ATCM and BID Foundation. It directly supported 138 high streets and delivered its Placemaking Programme in 32 places to over 800 stakeholders, with a 94% satisfaction rate.

In 2024 the UK Government introduced High Street Rental Auctions (HSRAs), giving local authorities the power to force landlords to rent out persistently vacant commercial units. The Institute of Place Management publicly stated that these powers will only work if local authorities have the capacity and local intelligence to use them. That capacity gap is exactly where COLHYBRI fits.

The UK ecosystem is also where COLHYBRI's financial infrastructure partners are concentrated: Ebury (London, IPO Q2 2026) powers cross-border payments for Fulham, Aston Villa and other Premier League clubs; Hummingbird Ventures (London) is a natural fit for pre-Series A; BID Foundation coordinates 340+ Business Improvement Districts across the country. First UK deployment targets: Manchester (near IPM HQ) and a London Business Improvement District.

context: La strategie Commerce Maroc 2030, lancee le 29 septembre 2025 par le Ministre Ryad Mezzour, pose quatre axes : couverture sociale des commercants, integration numerique, inclusion financiere et amelioration de l'approvisionnement. 300 000 commercants ont deja rejoint le systeme de Contribution Professionnelle Unifiee. Le programme Moroccan Retail Tech Builder (MRTB), porte avec l'UM6P et la Fondation OCP, accompagne 161 startups en phase 1 et vise 300 nouvelles startups en phase 2 (2025-2027).

La Coupe du Monde 2030, organisee conjointement par le Maroc, l'Espagne et le Portugal, est un accelerateur strategique pour la modernisation du commerce local. Les villes hotes marocaines (Casablanca, Rabat, Marrakech, Tanger, Agadir, Fes) vont voir converger flux touristiques, investissements infrastructures et digitalisation acceleree des commerces de proximite. COLHYBRI arrive au bon moment.

Les partenaires financiers sont deja mobilises : Attijariwafa Bank et Al Barid Bank proposent des solutions de paiement electronique adaptees aux commercants de proximite. La convention signee en avril 2025 entre le Ministere de l'Industrie et du Commerce et le Ministere de la Transition Numerique cree les centres regionaux du commerce electronique, infrastructure d'accueil naturelle pour le modele COLHYBRI.
